methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Hexium is a Seed-stage nuclear energy company founded by Charlie Jarrott and Jacob Peterson, having raised $10M+. The nuclear energy sector includes 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers, including established names like TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Due to limited Seed-stage peers within nuclear energy, peer-median valuation was not applicable. The analysis relied on stage-progression methodology, applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier from the $10M capital base to $25M. This represents the typical valuation lift observed in transitions from Seed to Series A across frontier-tech sectors.

The triangulated valuation range is $21M to $29M, with a median point of $25M. This range reflects uncertainty inherent to the single-method approach; a more robust triangulation would require either (i) identification of additional Seed or early-stage nuclear comparables with disclosed valuations, or (ii) relative revenue/burn multiples if operating metrics become available. Key sensitivities include the applicability of a 2.5x multiplier to early-stage nuclear ventures—a sector with longer development timelines and capital requirements than traditional software—and the absence of direct peer valuation anchors at this stage.
